headline: 'The presence of PFAS in my blood is out of my control': Six Europeans on living in 'forever pollution' hotspots
description: Being a victim is neither a profession nor their identity. Meta, Cristina, Doris, Stéphanie, Mari and Toon live in PFAS contamination hotspots in six European countries.

headline: Trump says Iran peace talks could take place 'over the next two days'

Hungary elections: 'Once the champagne has been popped, European leaders must reassess their expectations for the new government'
description: While the victory of Péter Magyar marks the end of illiberalism in Hungary, it should still be approached with caution, argue Piotr Buras and Pawel Zerka, both members of the European Council on Foreign Relations.

headline: The devastated residents of Chibab Tower, struck by four Israeli missiles
description: Israel targeted Ali Yusuf Harshi, the nephew and secretary of Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem. The attack killed 10 people in this 1970s apartment building, which stood as a symbol of Lebanon's ability to 'live together.'

headline: PSG reach Champions League semi-finals after Dembélé's double against Liverpool
description: Paris Saint-Germain, who won 2-0 at Anfield on Tuesday,  will now face Bayern Munich or 15-time winners Real Madrid in the last four. Luis Enrique's team remains on course to become the only side other than Madrid to retain the competition in the Champions League era.

headline: New online search tool in Germany reveals if ancestors were Nazi Party members
description: In partnership with American and German archives, the weekly 'Die Zeit' has launched a searchable tool granting access to more than 12 million documents, cataloging the bulk of Nazi Party (NSDAP) memberships from 1925 to 1945.

description: During the ruling, the president of the court drew an explicit connection between the 'commercial partnership' that the French cement company had entered with terrorist groups and the attacks that were then carried out by those groups.

headline: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
description: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

headline: American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
description: The US president claimed, against all plausibility, that the image he posted on social media showed him as a doctor, but he was forced to take it down. The post, along with his lengthy tirade against Leo XIV, left believers stunned.
